zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais於大約1470年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可沃爾辛厄姆時，他的父親Sir Gervase Clifton34歲，母親Alice Neville29歲。他與Ella Bennett在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1530年2月在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais于大约1470年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可沃爾辛厄姆时，他的父亲Sir Gervase Clifton34岁，母亲Alice Neville29岁。他与Ella Bennett在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1530年2月在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clifton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Nottingham,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Nottingham in the East Midlands. The placename means ‘the homestead of the people of Snot’, from the Old English personal name Snot + the groupname suffix -ingas (genitive -inga-) + hām ‘village, homestead’. The initial S- was lost in the 12th century, due to the influence of Anglo-Norman French (the combination sn- is alien to French).

nameMeanings.name: Treasurer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset): nickname from Middle English tresor ‘treasure, wealth, riches’ (Old French trésor, from Latin thesaurus ‘hoard’), for a treasurer or person in charge of financial administration, or as an affectionate nickname for a loved or valued person. This surname is also found in Monmouthshire, Wales.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Calais
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Onezime.
French: habitational name from the port of Calais or from a minor place of the same name in Sarthe.

variants.name: Callais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Octave, Orelia, Renette.
French: variant of   Calais  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais was born about 1470, in Walsingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Gervase Clifton, was 34 and his mother, Alice Neville, was 29. He married Ella Bennett in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about February 1530, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais est né environ 1470, à Walsingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Gervase Clifton, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Alice Neville, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Ella Bennett à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ février 1530, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais um 1470 in Walsingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Gervase Clifton 34 und seine Mutter Alice Neville 29. Er hat in England, United Kingdom Ella Bennett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um Februar 1530 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais è nato il circa 1470 nel Walsingham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Gervase Clifton, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Alice Neville, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Ella Bennett nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa febbraio 1530, nel Norwich, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calaisが約1470年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクワルシンガムで生まれた時，父Sir Gervase Cliftonは34歳，母Alice Nevilleは29歳であった。イギリスイギリスでElla Bennettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1530年2月にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais(이)가 대략 1470년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 월징함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Gervase Clifton(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Neville(은)는 29세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드에서 Ella Bennett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1530년 2월 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais nasceu aproximadamente 1470, em Walsingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Gervase Clifton, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Alice Neville, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Ella Bennett em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente fevereiro de 1530, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais родился около 1470 г. в местности валсингхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Gervase Clifton было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Neville, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Ella Bennett в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около февраль 1530 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Clifton, Sheriff of Nottingham, Treasurer of Calais nació aproximadamente 1470, en Walsingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Gervase Clifton, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Alice Neville, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Ella Bennett en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente febrero de 1530, en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 61 años.
